body.label: Definition 1
body.name: ToyPseudoLength
body.definition: Let G be a group. A function l : G -> \mathbb{R} is a toy pseudo-length if l(1)=0.

source.text: Let \(G\) be a group. A function \(l : G \to \mathbb{R}\) is a toy pseudo-length if \(l(1)=0\).
source.label: Definition 1

body.label: Definition 2
body.name: ToyLength
body.definition: Let G be a group. A toy pseudo-length l : G -> \mathbb{R} is a toy length if l(g)=0 implies g=1 for every g in G.

source.text: Let \(G\) be a group. A toy pseudo-length \(l : G \to \mathbb{R}\) is a toy length if \(l(g)=0\) implies \(g=1\) for every \(g\in G\).
source.label: Definition 2

hypothesis.assumption: For every g in G, l(g)=0.

source.text: Let \(G\) be a group and let \(l : G \to \mathbb{R}\) be a toy length. Assume that \(l(g)=0\) for every \(g\in G\). Then \(l(1)=0\).
source.label: Theorem 1

proof_steps.label: Auxiliary Lemma
proof_steps.name: zero_on_all_elements
proof_steps.claim: For every x in G, l(x)=0.

source.text: We first prove the auxiliary lemma that, for every \(x\in G\), \(l(x)=0\). This is just \(h_0\), applied to \(x\).
source.label: Auxiliary Lemma

proof_steps.claim: l(x)=0 for an arbitrary x in G.
proof_steps.proof_method: Apply the assumption that l(g)=0 for every g in G to x.
Apply the auxiliary lemma to x=1.
